properties.otherAdvice:
Changes to traffic conditions will be in place at various times southbound along the Warringah Fwy and other nearby roads as a result of the Warringah Freeway upgrade and Western Harbour Tunnel projects.

Changes to traffic conditions are also in place for northbound motorists - Warringah Fwy northbound.

From the 2nd of May 6:00am, major changes will be implemented to the Warringah Freeway to make it safer and easier to use. This may affect which lane you need to be in or the ramp you need to take. These changes include:

  • removal of the slip lane to Sydney Harbour Bridge
  • removal of access to Sydney Harbour Bridge from Ernest Street
  • change to ramp arrangements at Falcon Street/Military Road and removal of access to Cahill Expressway
  • opening of the new dedicated southbound bus lane along the Warringah Freeway
  • changes for overheight vehicles.

arrangementElements.html:
From the 2nd of May 6:00am the Ernest St on-ramp will no longer be able to access the Sydney Harbour Bridge and will become a dedicated Sydney Harbour Tunnel ramp only.

To access the Sydney Harbour Bridge from Ernest, motorists will need to use Miller St, Falcon St, Military Rd, or Mount St on-ramps instead.
arrangementElements.title: Ernest Street on-ramp

arrangementElements.html:
From the 2nd of May 6:00am the following changes to the Falcon St/Military Rd on-ramps will be implemented:

  • The eastern on-ramp will become a bus-only ramp.
  • The western on-ramp will only be able to access the Sydney Harbour Bridge and Tunnel.
  • Access to the Cahill Expressway and Alfred Street North off-ramp from this ramp will be removed.

To access Cahill Expressway, motorists can use the Miller St or Mount St on-ramps.
